concepts.name: Singly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where each element points to the next element.

concepts.name: Node
concepts.short_definition: The basic building block of a linked list, containing data and a pointer to the next node.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the first node in the linked list.

concepts.name: Next Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference within a node that points to the subsequent node in the list.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each node in a linked list sequentially.

concepts.name: Reversal
concepts.short_definition: The operation of changing the direction of pointers in a linked list.

concepts.name: Iterative Reversal
concepts.short_definition: Reversing a linked list using a loop and temporary pointers.

concepts.name: Recursive Reversal
concepts.short_definition: Reversing a linked list by calling a function on subsequent nodes.

concepts.name: Three Pointers
concepts.short_definition: A common technique using three pointers to manage node connections during iterative reversal.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the last node in the linked list. Useful for some reversal strategies.
